clean_story: Meaningful coincidences.  Physics tests the unseen links. "Synchronicity" describes events that align in meaning but lack a clear cause—like bumping into an old friend while thinking of them. Physics asks:Could hidden forces explain these?Quantum entanglement shows particles instantaneously mirror each other across vast distances—no signal, just correlation. Like two dice always landing the same number, no matter how far apart. Some theories suggest consciousness might interact with this fabric. In 2017, a study at Northwestern University found brainwaves of separated people briefly synced when one experienced strong emotions. Not telepathy—just measurable patterns. Others point to fractals:Nature repeats shapes (trees, coastlines) at different scales. Maybe synchronicity is life’s fractal math—echoes in the chaos. Three related subjects are quantum biology, emergent patterns and collective behavior.
